description:
Create a 'Hubble Palette' (SHO) color composite from three narrowband FITS files of the Eagle Nebula and export it as an RGB PNG image.

Expected Actions:
1. Open AstroImageJ.
2. Open the three narrowband FITS files located in `~/AstroImages/raw/eagle/`:
   - `502nmos.fits` (OIII emission)
   - `656nmos.fits` (H-alpha emission)
   - `673nmos.fits` (SII emission)
3. The raw FITS files are linear and appear mostly black. Stretch the display histogram (adjust brightness/contrast) of each image so the faint nebula structures become clearly visible.
4. Merge the three images into a single RGB color image using the standard Hubble Palette mapping:
   - Red channel: `673nmos.fits` (SII)
   - Green channel: `656nmos.fits` (H-alpha)
   - Blue channel: `502nmos.fits` (OIII)
5. Save the resulting color composite as a PNG file exactly at: `~/AstroImages/processed/eagle_hubble_palette.png`
